title: Unsung Witnesses of the Battle of Stalingrad
author_or_org: Olga Kucherenko / The National WWII Museum
pages_or_sections: Article discussion of evacuation and first Luftwaffe bombardment
strengths: Useful synthesis focused on civilians, with explicit Stalingrad population and evacuation context.
limitations: Secondary article rather than archival dataset; the 70,000 figure is presented as contested.
notes: Used for Stalingrad civilian numerator bounds and denominator context, not for military losses.

title: Stalingrad
date_scope: 23 August 1942 air raids and battle overview
strengths: Gives a clear lower-bound style direct civilian death figure tied to the first air raids.
limitations: The accessible web text is the item description, not the scanned page; use as supporting lower-bound evidence only.
notes: Used to anchor the low end of Stalingrad civilian direct deaths.

title: Stalingrad
author_or_org: Antony Beevor / text mirror hosted by The Ted K Archive
pages_or_sections: Chapter text on trapped civilians and evacuation after 23 August 1942
strengths: Provides explicit evacuation and residual-population figures useful for person-month modeling.
limitations: Mirror text is not a primary publisher source and should be treated as a locator / cross-check rather than the strongest citation tier.
notes: Used cautiously for denominator modeling and cross-checked against other Stalingrad sources.

title: When Titans Clashed: How the Red Army Stopped Hitler (2nd ed.)
author_or_org: David M. Glantz and Jonathan M. House
pages_or_sections: Table 9-1; Table 9-2; Table Q; notes to pages 149–172
geographic_scope: Stalingrad battle / Stalingrad region; also broader Soviet operations tables
date_scope: 17 July 1942 to 2 February 1943 and related notes
strengths: Most important military source here: provides Soviet strength snapshots, Soviet casualty tables, and Axis encirclement/death figures with citation to Krivosheev and related works.
limitations: Accessible copy is OCR text; some table parsing is messy. Several figures are battle- or region-level rather than city-only.
notes: Core source for the Stalingrad military numerator and denominator, but still requires modeling to convert mixed casualty categories into direct deaths.

title: Urban Warfare Project Case Study #1: Battle of Stalingrad
author_or_org: Modern War Institute at West Point
geographic_scope: Stalingrad city and surrounding battle area
strengths: Concise geography description of city layout and the west-bank urban strip.
limitations: Not a casualty dataset and not precise enough for numerators by itself.
notes: Used for geometry and theater description, not for primary numeric estimation.

title: The Theft of Historical Memory
pages_or_sections: Section summarizing the Leningrad City Commission evidence presented to Nuremberg
date_scope: Siege period, especially bombardment totals across the siege
strengths: Provides explicit direct bombardment civilian deaths and injuries, while separating them from starvation totals.
limitations: Secondary relay of tribunal-era evidence rather than a critical edition of the original commission documents.
notes: Useful for Leningrad direct civilian deaths from bombardment; not sufficient for city-matched military deaths.

title: Long-term consequences of war experiences on the development of children from the Siege of Leningrad
strengths: Clear statement of official trapped-population baseline, including children.
limitations: Not a demographic reconstruction; uses official background figures in a psychology article.
notes: Used for Leningrad baseline civilian presence only.

title: Toward a Central Database of Evacuated Soviet Children, 1941–1945
strengths: Provides concrete archival evacuation figures for Moscow and Leningrad with source notes.
limitations: Evacuation-focused article; does not itself provide city-matched combat death series.
notes: Used for displacement context in Moscow and Leningrad episodes.

title: The Siege of Leningrad, 1941–1944
author_or_org: Chapter in The Cambridge Companion to the Nazi-Soviet War
strengths: Useful high-level statement that starvation dominated and little fighting occurred inside the city itself.
limitations: Not a detailed casualty source for matched numerator/denominator work.
notes: Used to justify separating direct bombardment mortality from the much larger indirect starvation toll.

title: Battle of Moscow in 1941-2: The USSR's First Victory
pages_or_sections: Section on the Axis air offensive against Moscow
strengths: Provides a clean city-specific direct civilian death and wound count for Moscow air raids.
limitations: Secondary article quoting other historians; not sufficient for military denominators.
notes: Used to show that Moscow civilian direct deaths are observable even though city-matched military deaths are not.
